---
name: Bug Report
about: Create a report to help us improve the Dronzer Platform
title: "[BUG] "
labels: bug
assignees: ''

---

**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Configure Dronzer with `...`
2. Send a request to `...`
3. Observe error: `...`

**Expected behavior**
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.

**Actual behavior**
What actually happened? Include any error messages, stack traces, or unexpected responses.

**Environment**

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| OS | e.g. Ubuntu 22.04 / Windows 11 |
| Python Version | e.g. 3.13.1 |
| Dronzer Version | e.g. 2.0.0 |
| Deployment | e.g. Docker Compose / Kubernetes / Local |
| Provider Affected | e.g. Groq, Google AI Studio |

**Relevant Logs**
```
Paste any relevant log output or error messages here.
Include the request Trace ID if visible in the response headers (X-Trace-ID).
```

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here (e.g., does it affect all providers or a specific one? Does it happen on every request or intermittently?).
